message:
chore(beads,perf-artifacts): sync beads + include staged bd-cnk5d 2T cliff verify artifacts

Two unrelated sets of clean-up landed in a single commit because a
\`git add\` on .beads/issues.jsonl picked up changes a sibling
agent had already staged:

- .beads/issues.jsonl: routine \`br sync --flush-only\` export
  following the branchless visibility / page-buf capacity /
  projected rowid lookup batch.
- tests/artifacts/perf/bd-cnk5d-2t-cliff-verify-20260424/**:
  captured benchmark outputs from the bd-cnk5d 2-thread cliff
  verification run (bench-2t-result.txt, bench-stderr.log,
  bench-stdout.log, bench-sweep.log, head.txt, hostinfo.txt,
  perf-top.txt, perf-top20.txt, summary.md). These are frozen
  artifacts under tests/artifacts/perf/ and are tracked in-repo
  per the project's perf-evidence convention. They were already
  staged by the sibling agent; this commit records them rather
  than orphaning them in the index.

No product code changes.

 {"id":"bd-bt16","title":"§6.1-6.2 ARC Cache Rationale + MVCC-Aware Data Structures","description":"Implements §6.1-6.2 of the FrankenSQLite spec: ARC cache rationale and MVCC-aware data structures for the buffer pool.

SUMMARY: Establishes why ARC (Adaptive Replacement Cache, Megiddo & Modha FAST '03) replaces LRU for database workloads, and defines the MVCC-aware keying scheme where cache entries are indexed by (PageNumber, CommitSeq) instead of PageNumber alone. Covers core data structures (CacheKey, CachedPage, EntryRef, RecencyStore, GhostStore, ArcCache), implementation strategy (slab-allocated intrusive doubly-linked lists for exact ARC, or CAR clock buffers), and concurrency model (Mutex for ARC ops, AtomicU32 ref_count for lock-free read-side pinning).

KEY DATA STRUCTURES AND ALGORITHMS:
- CacheKey: {pgno: PageNumber, commit_seq: CommitSeq} — commit_seq=0 is on-disk baseline; uncommitted pages live in txn write_set, NOT in ARC
- CachedPage: {key, data: PageData, ref_count: AtomicU32, xxh3: Xxh3Hash, byte_size: usize, wal_frame: Option<u32>}
- ArcCache: {t1: RecencyStore, t2: RecencyStore, b1: GhostStore, b2: GhostStore, p: usize, capacity, total_bytes, max_bytes, index: HashMap<CacheKey, EntryRef>}
- RecencyStore<K,V>: T1/T2 lists with front/pop_front/push_back/move_to_back/rotate_front_to_back ops
- GhostStore<K>: B1/B2 metadata-only lists with contains/remove/push_back/pop_front ops
- Implementation: slab-allocated intrusive lists (exact ARC) or CAR clock buffers (Bansal & Modha FAST '04)

ARC ADVANTAGE PATTERNS: (1) Scan-then-point — scan enters T1 only, hot pages stay in T2; (2) Frequency skew (Zipfian) — frequent pages promoted to T2; (3) Loop patterns — ghost hits in B1 adjust p for partial hit rate. Patent US 6,996,676 expired Feb 2024.

NORMATIVE INVARIANTS:
- MUST NOT evict pinned pages (ref_count > 0)
- Eviction MUST NOT perform I/O (no WAL append, no durability I/O — pure memory operation)
- MUST prefer evicting superseded versions (newer committed version exists visible to all active snapshots)
- All ArcCache operations protected by Mutex; CachedPage.ref_count uses atomics for lock-free read-side
- Transaction-private (uncommitted) page images are NOT ARC cache entries

UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS:
1. test_cache_key_mvcc_awareness: Different (pgno, commit_seq) pairs are different keys
2. test_arc_t1_t2_promotion: First access places in T1, second access promotes to T2
3. test_arc_ghost_hit_b1: B1 hit increases p (favor recency)
4. test_arc_ghost_hit_b2: B2 hit decreases p (favor frequency)
5. test_scan_resistance: Full-table scan does not evict hot working set from T2
6. test_pinned_page_not_evicted: ref_count > 0 prevents eviction
7. test_eviction_no_io: Eviction path performs zero I/O calls
8. test_superseded_version_preferred: Newer committed version causes old version to be evicted first
9. test_memory_accounting: total_bytes tracks correctly across inserts and evictions

E2E TEST: test_e2e_arc_mvcc_integration_smoke — run concurrent transactions reading/writing pages with ARC active; verify ARC never evicts pinned pages and liveness holds (no deadlock from all-pinned preferred list).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- ARC data structures compile and pass all 9 unit tests
- MVCC keying correctly differentiates page versions by commit_seq
- Eviction respects all three normative constraints (no pinned eviction, no I/O, prefer superseded)
- Memory accounting tracks total_bytes accurately within 1% tolerance
- Concurrent pin/unpin transitions are race-free under stress test","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:02:58.826817832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:20:41.026933391Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:20:41.026909777Z","close_reason":"Implemented ARC MVCC-aware cache structures + required unit/E2E tests in fsqlite-pager","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bt16","depends_on_id":"bd-1cqs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-bt16","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-bt16","depends_on_id":"bd-7pu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1717,"issue_id":"bd-bt16","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §6.1-6.2 ARC Cache Rationale + MVCC-Aware Data Structures

### Spec Content (Lines 10614-10792)

**Why ARC not LRU:** LRU fails catastrophically for DB workloads (single table scan evicts entire working set). ARC (Megiddo & Modha, FAST '03) auto-tunes between recency and frequency. Patent US 6,996,676 expired Feb 2024 — legally safe.

Three canonical failure patterns where ARC dominates:
1. Scan-then-point: scan enters T1, hot pages stay in T2
2. Frequency skew (Zipfian): frequent pages promoted to T2
3. Loop patterns: ghost hits in B1 adjust p for partial hit rate

**MVCC-Aware Keying:** Standard ARC keys on PageNumber. Our variant keys on (PageNumber, CommitSeq) because multiple versions coexist.

**Core Types:**
- CacheKey: {pgno: PageNumber, commit_seq: CommitSeq}
  - commit_seq=0 = on-disk baseline (NOT uncommitted — uncommitted live in txn write_set)
- CachedPage: {key, data: PageData, ref_count: AtomicU32, xxh3: Xxh3Hash, byte_size, wal_frame: Option<u32>}
- ArcCache: {t1: RecencyStore, t2: RecencyStore, b1: GhostStore, b2: GhostStore, p: usize, capacity, total_bytes, max_bytes, index: HashMap<CacheKey, EntryRef>}

**Implementation options:**
1. Exact ARC (recommended baseline): slab-allocated intrusive doubly-linked lists (NOT LinkedHashMap)
2. CAR (optional): Clock with Adaptive Replacement (Bansal & Modha, FAST '04) — clock buffers instead of linked lists

**Concurrency:** All ArcCache operations under Mutex. CachedPage.ref_count uses atomics for lock-free read-side. Mutex critical section is short (clock sweep is sequential).

**Eviction constraints:**
1. Never evict pinned page (ref_count > 0)
2. Eviction MUST NOT perform I/O (no WAL append, no durability I/O)
3. Prefer superseded versions

### Unit Tests Required
1. test_cache_key_mvcc_awareness: Different (pgno, commit_seq) are different keys
2. test_arc_t1_t2_promotion: First access → T1, second → T2
3. test_arc_ghost_hit_b1: B1 hit increases p (favor recency)
4. test_arc_ghost_hit_b2: B2 hit decreases p (favor frequency)
5. test_scan_resistance: Full-table scan doesn't evict hot working set
6. test_pinned_page_not_evicted: ref_count > 0 prevents eviction
7. test_eviction_no_io: Eviction path performs zero I/O calls
8. test_superseded_version_preferred: Newer committed version → old version evicted first
9. test_memory_accounting: total_bytes tracks correctly
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:05:50Z"},{"id":1718,"issue_id":"bd-bt16","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Merged from P2 bead bd-1lcf (§6.1-6.2 Why ARC + MVCC-Aware Data Structures)

## §6.1 Why ARC, Not LRU

LRU fails catastrophically for DB workloads: a single table scan evicts the entire working set. ARC (Adaptive Replacement Cache, Megiddo & Modha, FAST '03) auto-tunes between recency and frequency. The ARC paper proves 2c-entry directory always contains the c pages LRU(c) would retain. Competitive ratio for deterministic paging is k (cache size), not 2 — ARC's contribution is adaptive self-tuning.

**Patent note:** ARC patent (US 6,996,676 B2) expired Feb 2024 — implementation is legally safe.

**Three canonical advantage patterns:**
1. **Scan-then-point:** Scan touches every page once — enters T1 but never promotes to T2. Hot pages remain in T2 untouched. LRU evicts all hot pages.
2. **Frequency skew (Zipfian 10/90):** LRU can't distinguish 1-access vs 1000-access pages. ARC promotes frequent pages to T2, protecting from recency-only eviction.
3. **Loop patterns:** Working set slightly larger than cache — LRU gets 0% hit rate. ARC detects looping via B1 ghost hits, adjusts p for partial hit rate.

## §6.2 MVCC-Aware ARC Data Structures

Standard ARC keys on page number. Our variant keys on (PageNumber, CommitSeq) because multiple versions coexist.

**CacheKey:** `{ pgno: PageNumber, commit_seq: CommitSeq }` — commit_seq=0 is on-disk baseline. Transaction-private images are NOT ARC entries; they live in owning transaction's write_set.

**CachedPage:** `{ key: CacheKey, data: PageData, ref_count: AtomicU32, xxh3: Xxh3Hash, byte_size: usize, wal_frame: Option<u32> }`

**EntryRef:** Implementation-specific handle into T1/T2. Exact ARC: NodeIdx in slab. CAR: SlotIdx in clock buffer.

**RecencyStore<K,V>:** T1/T2. Ops: membership probe, front/pop_front/push_back/move_to_back/rotate_front_to_back.

**GhostStore<K>:** B1/B2 (metadata-only). Ops: contains/remove/push_back/pop_front.

**ArcCache:** t1/t2 (RecencyStore), b1/b2 (GhostStore), p (adaptive target T1 size), capacity, total_bytes, max_bytes, index (HashMap<CacheKey, EntryRef>).

**Implementation (Extreme Optimization):** DO NOT use LinkedHashMap. Prefer slab-allocated intrusive lists (exact ARC) or CAR clock buffers (Bansal & Modha FAST '04). CAR: sequential memory sweep, CPU prefetcher friendly, eliminates pointer churn. All ops protected by Mutex; ref_count is atomic for lock-free reads.

**Eviction Constraints (normative):**
1. Never evict pinned (ref_count > 0)
2. Eviction is pure memory — MUST NOT append to .wal, MUST NOT perform durability I/O
3. Prefer superseded versions (newer committed version exists, visible to all active snapshots)
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:24:36Z"},{"id":1719,"issue_id":"bd-bt16","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## E2E Test

- **test_e2e_arc_mvcc_integration_smoke**:
  - Run concurrent transactions that read and write pages while ARC is active.
  - Verify ARC never evicts pinned pages (ref_count > 0) and liveness holds (no deadlock due to all-pinned preferred list).

## Logging Requirements

- DEBUG: pin/unpin transitions: `page`, `ref_count`, `txn_id`.
- DEBUG: ARC entry state: `key`, `state` (Ready|Loading), `list` (T1|T2|B1|B2).

 {"id":"bd-byzpb","title":"H2: Batch record encoding — encode N rows into a contiguous buffer in one pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:26:07.805123347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T19:50:09.013042632Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-byzpb","depends_on_id":"bd-sup6g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:26:08.330823452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3372,"issue_id":"bd-byzpb","author":"ubuntu","text":"## What
For multi-row INSERT VALUES (...), (...), (...), instead of encoding one record at a time, batch-encode all rows into a contiguous buffer. This enables:
1. Single allocation for all records
2. Amortized header computation
3. Better CPU cache utilization (sequential writes)

## Alien Graveyard Reference: §4.3 Vectorized Execution (batch processing)

## Where
- crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs: Detect multi-row VALUES and emit BatchMakeRecord opcode
- crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs: BatchMakeRecord handler encodes all rows in one loop

## Expected Impact: -0.05 to -0.10 µs per row for multi-row inserts

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Multi-row INSERT benchmark ≥ 20% faster
2. Correct for mixed-type columns
3. Single allocation for entire batch (verify with heaptrack)
","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:26:08Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-c0bsc","title":"Track W: Reclaim prepared DML fast lanes and mixed OLTP performance","description":"Track W now centers on reclaiming existing prepared DML fast lanes, fixing same-connection mixed read/write overlap costs, explicitly linking to autocommit-retention and mixed-OLTP prepared-query work, and tying the plan to Track T plus bd-wwqen.* rather than assuming a standalone micro-optimization plan.","acceptance_criteria":"Keep concurrent mode default on; materially improve autocommit, mixed OLTP, and key single-thread DML benchmark cells; preserve conformance and concurrent-writer wins; ship deterministic unit/e2e proof artifacts with structured logs.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T14:34:08.491648036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:56:21.706084469Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc","depends_on_id":"bd-25zqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:04:27.149017070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc","depends_on_id":"bd-aw0b9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:14:01.751373403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc","depends_on_id":"bd-wwqen.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:04:27.417751460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc","depends_on_id":"bd-wwqen.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:14:02.030186599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3459,"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Corrected Impact Estimates (third-pass review)

### Revised per-row savings:
| Bead | Original est. | Corrected est. | Notes |
|------|-------------|----------------|-------|
| W1 | 200ns | **200ns** | Verified — engine ceremony elimination is real |
| W2 | 500ns | **100ns** | Corrected — W1 already handles ceremony; W2 only saves dispatch |
| W3 | 60ns | **60ns** | Verified — reload_page_fresh is real waste |
| W4 | 60ns | **20ns** | Reduced — J1 already eliminated decode, only dirty-tracking remains |
| W5 | 100ns | **100ns** | Verified — dispatch chain collapse is real |
| W6 | 30ns | **30ns** | Verified — HashMap cache hit is real |
| W7 | CLOSED | 0ns | Duplicate of Track I (already implemented) |
| W8 | 300ns | **200ns** | Inherits from W1+W5, not W2 (direct UPDATE/DELETE is risky) |

### Revised total: ~710ns per row (was ~950ns)
### At 100K rows: ~71ms saved (was ~95ms)
### Revised gap: 4.5µs → ~3.8µs → ~12.7x (was ~9.5x target)

### Honest assessment:
Track W alone will NOT close the gap to 2-3x. The remaining ~3.5µs after W is:
- B-tree/pager MVCC tax: ~300-500ns (fundamental — page copies, write_set)
- PageData Arc overhead: ~90-250ns (needs PageData redesign — Track X territory)
- Cache/indirection effects: ~1000ns (systemic — larger working set than C SQLite)

To reach 2-3x would require changing the page data model (Arc→buffer pool) which is a much larger effort.

### Realistic Track W target: 18x → ~13x (not 2-3x)
","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:32:30Z"},{"id":3461,"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Context

Track W should match the codebase and benchmark artifacts that exist today, not the earlier mental model.

### Already true on current head
- Retained autocommit machinery already exists for `:memory:` and file-backed paths.
- Lazy MemDB dirty tracking already exists; per-row MemDB decode is not the main remaining cost.
- The prepared direct simple INSERT lane already exists.
- Prepared UPDATE/DELETE fast-lane machinery already exists.
- Reusable VDBE engine + retained cursor reuse already exist on the reusable table-program lane.

### Current pain points from the checked-in benchmark artifact
- `10000 rows / autocommit`: ~35x slower
- `100000 rows / batched (1000/txn)`: ~43x slower
- `100000 rows / single txn`: ~12.5x slower
- `100000 rows / update 10000 rows`: ~15.4x slower
- `100000 rows / delete 5000 rows`: ~14.1x slower
- `Mixed OLTP Workload (80% read / 20% write)`: ~22x slower

### Revised diagnosis
The highest-EV gaps are not "invent a brand-new INSERT bypass" or "skip 20ns of dirty-tracking bookkeeping".

The leading suspects are:
1. same-connection read-after-write behavior forcing retained-autocommit flush/reload on overlapping reads
2. direct prepared INSERT truth-matrix drift between the existing direct lane, its gating helper, and benchmark-shape tests
3. incomplete `BTREE_APPEND` / rightmost-leaf parity for sequential INSERT workloads
4. residual ceremony on reusable prepared DML lanes
5. structural `PageData = Arc<Vec<u8>>` and `write_set_data = Arc<HashMap<...>>` tax, which belongs to the parity architecture tracks rather than ad-hoc micro-beads

## Track W strategy

### P0 spear tip
- Reclaim the existing direct prepared INSERT lane for pure-parameter / benchmark-style shapes
- Add same-connection read-your-own-write overlay so overlapping reads on the same connection do not force global flush + full MemDB reload when the write-set can satisfy correctness
- Treat Track T (`BTREE_APPEND` parity) and `bd-wwqen.3` (prepared-DML overhead / bulk INSERT fast path) as linked implementation tracks, not unrelated side quests

### P1 support work
- Trim residual reusable-VDBE setup on the reusable lane
- Collapse residual dispatch ceremony on reusable prepared DML paths
- Finish the remaining non-append `reload_page_fresh` cleanup where the append path is already optimized
- Audit and extend the existing UPDATE/DELETE fast lane instead of building a duplicate bypass

### P2 backlog cleanup
- Dirty-tracking bookkeeping trims
- Safe write-set locality hints

### Out of scope for Track W
Large structural parity work such as flat page maps, zero-alloc record encoding, and register/value layout belongs to the existing parity architecture epic (`bd-e1hty`) and its child tracks.
","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:04:00Z"},{"id":3462,"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc","author":"ubuntu","text":"- `concurrent_mode_default` stays ON and no serialized file-lock bottleneck is reintroduced.
- Pure-parameter prepared INSERT benchmark shapes use the intended direct lane, with explicit truth-matrix tests and profiling counters.
- Same-connection interleaved write/read workloads no longer pay an avoidable retained-autocommit flush + full MemDB reload on overlapping reads.
- Mixed OLTP benchmark and the main single-thread DML benchmark cells show material improvement relative to the current checked-in artifact.
- Concurrent-writer benchmarks do not regress materially.
- 938/938 conformance remains green, plus detailed unit/e2e logging for each new fast path and fallback.
","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:04:00Z"},{"id":3481,"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc","author":"ubuntu","text":"Backlog surgery complete: Track W is now aligned to the live code and checked-in benchmark artifact rather than the earlier stale model.","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:04:27Z"},{"id":3487,"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc","author":"ubuntu","text":"Track W direct-lane work is now explicitly defined as a prepare-time INSERT lane classifier plus compiled direct row-builder/template work, not a one-bit heuristic flip.","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:07:36Z"},{"id":3488,"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc","author":"ubuntu","text":"User-centered revision: Track W now explicitly depends on the active retained-autocommit and mixed-OLTP prepared-query work because those are still visible benchmark pain points.","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:14:04Z"},{"id":3505,"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Authoritative planning note

Earlier comments on Track W include historical estimates and interim benchmark interpretations from older sessions. The authoritative plan is now:

Track W should match the codebase and benchmark artifacts that exist today, not the earlier mental model.

### Already true on current head
- Retained autocommit machinery already exists for `:memory:` and file-backed paths.
- Lazy MemDB dirty tracking already exists; per-row MemDB decode is not the main remaining cost.
- The prepared direct simple INSERT lane already exists.
- Prepared UPDATE/DELETE fast-lane machinery already exists.
- Reusable VDBE engine + retained cursor reuse already exist on the reusable table-program lane.

### Current pain points from the checked-in benchmark artifact
- `10000 rows / autocommit`: ~35x slower
- `100000 rows / batched (1000/txn)`: ~43x slower
- `100000 rows / single txn`: ~12.5x slower
- `100000 rows / update 10000 rows`: ~15.4x slower
- `100000 rows / delete 5000 rows`: ~14.1x slower
- `Mixed OLTP Workload (80% read / 20% write)`: ~22x slower

### Revised diagnosis
The highest-EV gaps are not "invent a brand-new INSERT bypass" or "skip 20ns of dirty-tracking bookkeeping".

The leading suspects are:
1. same-connection read-after-write behavior forcing retained-autocommit flush/reload on overlapping reads
2. direct prepared INSERT truth-matrix drift between the existing direct lane, its gating helper, and benchmark-shape tests
3. incomplete `BTREE_APPEND` / rightmost-leaf parity for sequential INSERT workloads
4. residual ceremony on reusable prepared DML lanes
5. structural `PageData = Arc<Vec<u8>>` and `write_set_data = Arc<HashMap<...>>` tax, which belongs to the parity architecture tracks rather than ad-hoc micro-beads

## Track W strategy

### P0 spear tip
- Reclaim the existing direct prepared INSERT lane for pure-parameter / benchmark-style shapes
- Add same-connection read-your-own-write overlay so overlapping reads on the same connection do not force global flush + full MemDB reload when the write-set can satisfy correctness
- Treat Track T (`BTREE_APPEND` parity) and `bd-wwqen.3` (prepared-DML overhead / bulk INSERT fast path) as linked implementation tracks, not unrelated side quests

### P1 support work
- Trim residual reusable-VDBE setup on the reusable lane
- Collapse residual dispatch ceremony on reusable prepared DML paths
- Finish the remaining non-append `reload_page_fresh` cleanup where the append path is already optimized
- Audit and extend the existing UPDATE/DELETE fast lane instead of building a duplicate bypass

### P2 backlog cleanup
- Dirty-tracking bookkeeping trims
- Safe write-set locality hints

### Out of scope for Track W
Large structural parity work such as flat page maps, zero-alloc record encoding, and register/value layout belongs to the existing parity architecture epic (`bd-e1hty`) and its child tracks.

 {"id":"bd-c0bsc.1","title":"W9: Same-connection read-your-own-write overlay — stop retained-autocommit flush/reload on overlapping SELECT","description":"Add a same-connection private-view/overlay path so overlapping reads do not force avoidable retained-autocommit flush plus full MemDB reload.","acceptance_criteria":"Same-connection overlapping reads stop forcing avoidable flush/reload; cross-connection commit visibility remains correct; mixed-OLTP microbench improves; unit + e2e overlap scenarios emit flush/overlay counters and traceable artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:04:23.157413937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:32:27.190101710Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T23:32:27.189768126Z","close_reason":"Implemented and verified same-connection retained-autocommit overlay on ts2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc.1","depends_on_id":"bd-aw0b9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:14:02.301135980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc.1","depends_on_id":"bd-c0bsc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:04:23.157413937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3479,"issue_id":"bd-c0bsc.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"## What
Eliminate the same-connection mixed-workload collapse caused by forcing retained-autocommit flush + full MemDB reload before overlapping reads when the same connection could satisfy correctness from its private uncommitted write-set / snapshot state.

## Why
The current checked-in benchmark artifact shows a severe regression on mixed OLTP workloads. The existing retained-autocommit machinery appears to pay a large penalty when a read on the same connection overlaps dirty tables and triggers flush/reload behavior.

## Scope
- add a same-connection read-your-own-write overlay or equivalent private-view mechanism
- keep SQLite semantics for other connections: only committed data is externally visible
- preserve correctness for file-backed and `:memory:` paths
- avoid turning this into a global visibility change or a concurrency-mode regression

## Guardrails
- same-connection optimization only
- no weakening of isolation or durability semantics for other connections
- no fallback to serialized file-lock behavior

W9 should not ship as a vague “overlay exists” optimization. It needs a written flush-decision matrix that future implementers can test mechanically.

### Overlay may satisfy reads only when all are true
- same connection as the retained autocommit writer
- read shape is explicitly supported by the current phase
- no statement class that must observe globally committed state first
- no schema / pragma / transaction-boundary rule requiring publication

### Force flush/publication when any apply
- another connection must observe the write
- explicit `BEGIN` / `COMMIT` / rollback boundary requires it
- DDL / `PRAGMA` / `VACUUM` / `ATTACH` / `DETACH`
- unsupported read shape
- connection close / reopen visibility contract

### Required proof
Every branch in that matrix needs a named unit or e2e scenario and a logged `flush_reason` or `overlay_reason`.

 {"id":"bd-c1fst","title":"Track U: Dirty-Page Bitmap — Replace Group Commit Overhead with C SQLite sqlite3PagerWrite Pattern","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:33:25.903305833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:33:28.735979112Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c1fst","depends_on_id":"bd-c9pxw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:33:28.735233443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c1fst","depends_on_id":"bd-iwux3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:33:28.321172007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3445,"issue_id":"bd-c1fst","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Problem (from C SQLite analysis: pager.c:6234-6250)
C SQLite's sqlite3PagerWrite() is a 2-cycle operation: check if page is already dirty, if so return immediately. First write to a page: journal the original, mark dirty. Subsequent writes: instant return.

FrankenSQLite's write path involves group commit coordination with Mutex + Condvar (pager.rs:38-150). Even single-threaded writes pay for group commit queue management. Cost: 100-500 cycles per write.

## Solution: Dirty-page tracking with deferred commit
1. On page write: just set a bit in a bitmap (1 CPU cycle)
2. Collect dirty pages in a Vec during transaction
3. At COMMIT: flush all dirty pages to WAL in one batch
4. Skip group commit coordination entirely for single-connection mode

## C SQLite Reference: pager.c:6234
```c
int sqlite3PagerWrite(PgHdr *pPg){
    if( (pPg->flags & PGHDR_WRITEABLE)!=0 && pPager->dbSize>=pPg->pgno ){
        return SQLITE_OK;  // Already dirty — instant return!
    }
    // ... first-write path (journal + mark dirty)
}
```

## Expected Impact: -100 to -500 cycles per row for write operations
For UPDATE/DELETE (which modify existing pages): most pages are already dirty → instant return.

## Where
- crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs (write path, group commit)
- New: dirty_bitmap: Vec<u64> (1 bit per page, 8KB covers 64K pages)
","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:33:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-c6tx","title":"§5.10.6-5.10.8 History Compression & Merge Certificates","description":"Implement PageHistory objects for MVCC compression (§5.10.6), intent commutativity rules (trace-normalized merge, §5.10.7), and MergeCertificate generation/verification (§5.10.8). Includes the independent-op definition.

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS
- test_page_history_newest_full_image_older_patches: Verify newest committed version is full page image; older versions stored as intent log patches or structured patches
- test_page_history_ecs_encode_decode_roundtrip: PageHistory object survives ECS encode/decode with lossless patch chain restoration
- test_intent_independence_relation: For all IntentOp pairs, verify independence (a,b) in I_intent iff: same schema_epoch, both structural==NONE, disjoint Writes, no Write-Read overlap
- test_update_expression_column_disjoint_commutativity: Two UpdateExpressions on same (table, key) with disjoint ColumnIdx sets are independent; overlapping ColumnIdx sets are not
- test_join_max_int_update_recognized: Verify is_join_max_int_update detects max(col, c) canonical forms in both argument orders; verify multiple join-max updates collapse to single with c=max(c_1, c_2)
- test_merge_certificate_generation_and_verification: Successful merge produces MergeCertificate with correct op_digests, footprint_digest, normal_form, and post_state page_hashes
- test_merge_certificate_replay_deterministic: Given (base snapshot, intents, certificate), re-execute parse->merge->repack; verify page_hashes and btree_invariant_hash match certificate
- test_circuit_breaker_disables_merging_on_verification_failure: Corrupt a certificate field; verify verification fails, SAFE merging disabled (write_merge=OFF), evidence ledger entry emitted

## E2E TEST
test_e2e_history_compression_preserves_query_results: Generate 100-commit write history on hot pages; compress per PageHistory spec; verify queries at selected commit_seq points return identical results before and after compression within retention window.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] PageHistory objects store newest version as full image and older versions as patches (bounded memory)
- [ ] Independence relation correctly classifies all IntentOp pair combinations per trace-monoid formalization

### Spec Content (Lines 10441-10613)

**§5.10.6 PageHistory Objects:**
Compressed version chains stored as ECS objects: newest = full image, older = patches (intent logs and/or XOR deltas). This enables bounded storage while maintaining full MVCC history for active snapshots.

**§5.10.7 Intent Footprints and Commutativity (Trace-Normalized Merge):**
Uses Mazurkiewicz trace theory (§4.4) to formalize when intent operations commute:
- Define independence relation I ⊆ IntentOp × IntentOp
- Two ops are independent iff they can be swapped without changing the outcome
- Trace equivalence: two intent logs are equivalent if they produce the same result under any reordering consistent with I
This provides a rigorous foundation for the merge safety ladder (§5.10.4).

**§5.10.8 Merge Certificates (Proof-Carrying Merge):**
Every successful merge produces a MergeWitness (ECS object) containing:
- The original intent logs from both transactions
- The independence relation used
- The merged page image
- A proof that the merge is equivalent to some serial execution
This makes merges auditable and replicable.

### Unit Tests Required
1. test_page_history_compression: Full image + patches correct
2. test_page_history_ecs_round_trip: PageHistory survives ECS encode/decode
3. test_intent_commutativity: All IntentOp pairs tested for independence
4. test_trace_equivalence: Equivalent traces produce same state
5. test_merge_certificate_generation: MergeWitness produced on successful merge
6. test_merge_certificate_replay: MergeWitness sufficient to re-derive merge

PURPOSE: Implement merge verification proofs, MVCC history compression, trace-normalized commutativity analysis, and merge certificates.

## §5.10.5 What Must Be Proven
Runnable proofs (proptest + DPOR), NOT prose:
- B-tree invariants hold after replay/merge: ordering, cell count bounds, free space, overflow chain
- Patch algebra: apply(p, merge(a,b)) == apply(apply(p,a), b) when mergeable; commutativity for commutative ops
- Determinism: identical (intent_log, base_snapshot) → identical outcome under LabRuntime across seeds
- UpdateExpression determinism: evaluate_rebase_expr(expr, row) deterministic for (expr, row) pair
- Expression safety: expr_is_rebase_safe correctly rejects all non-deterministic/side-effectful expressions

## §5.10.6 MVCC History Compression: PageHistory Objects
- Full page images per version is unacceptable long-term
- Strategy:
  - Newest committed version: full page image (fast reads)
  - Older versions: patches (intent logs and/or structured patches)
  - Hot pages: encode patch chains as ECS PageHistory objects → repairable + remotely fetchable
- This is how MVCC avoids eating memory under real write concurrency

## §5.10.7 Intent Footprints and Commutativity (Trace-Normalized Merge)

### Independence Relation on Intents (normative, trace-monoid formalization)
Two intent ops a, b are independent (a,b) ∈ I_intent iff:
- a.schema_epoch == b.schema_epoch, AND
- a.footprint.structural == NONE AND b.footprint.structural == NONE, AND
- Writes(a) ∩ Writes(b) = ∅, AND
- Writes(a) ∩ Reads(b) = ∅ AND Writes(b) ∩ Reads(a) = ∅

SAFE merges additional restriction:
- Reads(a) AND Reads(b) MUST both be empty
  - UpdateExpression implicit column reads in RebaseExpr (NOT in footprint.reads) → condition satisfied
  - Uniqueness checks re-validated during replay → NOT in footprint.reads

### UpdateExpression Commutativity Refinement
- Two UpdateExpressions on same (table, key) have overlapping Writes at SemanticKeyRef level
- Column-level override: independent iff columns_written(a) ∩ columns_written(b) = ∅
- If any column index overlaps → NOT independent (sub-row granularity conflict)

### Join-Update Exception (normative, REQUIRED for AUTOINCREMENT)
- Some overlapping column updates commute by algebra (not disjointness)
- V1 permits exactly one class: monotone join updates col = max(col, c) on INTEGER
- is_join_max_int_update(col_idx, expr) detects canonical forms:
  - MAX(ColumnRef(col_idx), Literal(Integer(c))) -- either argument order
- Two UpdateExpressions with overlapping ColumnIdx are independent if ALL overlapping columns satisfy is_join_max_int_update
- Deterministic normalization: multiple join-max updates → collapse to single with c = max(c_1, c_2, ...)
  - Justified: max is associative, commutative, idempotent on integers

### UpdateExpression + materialized Update/Delete on same key → NEVER independent

### Canonical Merge Order (normative)
- Sigma_intent: alphabet of intent ops identified by op_digest (Trunc128(BLAKE3('fsqlite:intent:v1' || bytes)))
- Foata normal form layering; within each layer sort by (btree_id, kind, key_digest, op_kind, op_digest)
- This exact order recorded in merge certificate (§5.10.8)

### Mergeable Intent Classes (normative, deliberately narrow)
- Insert/Delete/Update on table B-tree leaf pages for DISTINCT RowId keys (no overflow, no multi-page balance)
- UpdateExpression on table B-tree leaf pages (column-disjointness rule)
- IndexInsert/IndexDelete on index B-tree leaf pages for DISTINCT index keys (no overflow, no balance)
- Any op with structural \!= NONE → non-commutative → abort/retry only

### Key Identity Alignment (REQUIRED)
- StructuredPagePatch.cell_ops.cell_key_digest MUST use same domain-separated semantic key digest as SemanticKeyRef.key_digest
- Merge machinery MUST NOT treat physical offsets as identity

## §5.10.8 Merge Certificates (Proof-Carrying Merge)

### Requirement
- Any commit via merge path MUST produce verifiable MergeCertificate
- Native mode: attached to CommitProof (referenced by marker record)
- Compatibility mode: emitted to evidence ledger, MAY persist to sidecar

### MergeCertificate Schema (normative)
- merge_kind: { rebase, structured_patch, rebase+patch }
- base_commit_seq: u64, schema_epoch: u64
- pages: Vec<PageNumber>
- intent_op_digests: Vec<[u8;16]> -- ops involved
- footprint_digest: [u8;16] -- digest over all IntentFootprints
- normal_form: Vec<[u8;16]> -- op digests in canonical order used
- post_state: { page_hashes: Vec<(PageNumber, [u8;16])>, btree_invariant_hash: [u8;16] }
- verifier_version: u32

### Verification Algorithm (normative)
Given (base snapshot, intents, certificate):
1. Recompute all op_digest values from canonical intent encodings
2. Recompute footprint_digest from IntentFootprint values
3. Check normal_form is valid trace-monoid normal form under I_intent
4. Re-execute parse → merge → repack for affected pages + B-tree invariants
5. Compare page_hashes and btree_invariant_hash

### Circuit Breaker (normative)
If any merge verification fails → correctness incident:
- Production: disable SAFE merging (PRAGMA write_merge = OFF), emit evidence ledger entry, escalate supervision
- Lab mode: fail fast (test failure)

PARENT EPIC: bd-3t3
DEPENDS ON: bd-1h3b (Rebase + Physical Merge + Policy), bd-2blq (Intent Logs)

Dependencies:
  -> bd-3t3 (parent-child) - §5: MVCC Formal Model (Revised)
  -> bd-1h3b (blocks) - §5.10.2-5.10.4 Deterministic Rebase + Physical Merge + Merge Policy
  -> bd-2blq (blocks) - §5.10.1-5.10.1.1 Intent Logs + RowId Allocation in Concurrent Mode

+## Interpretation
+
+1. **Recovery-fence fix worked on its stated target.** Where it shows
+   up: 12t `BusyRecovery` failures are gone (cod_5's finding), 1t
+   throughput is at the post-db-generation-cache-fix "typical" band
+   (~240k wps), and no run in this sweep observed the 100 ms sleep
+   penalty during Connection::open. See `0f04cb25` commit body.
+2. **2t cliff is NOT the recovery fence.** 2t fs_wps moved from the
+   8,918 campaign-summary baseline to 9,554 — +7% total, within run
+   variance. This was predicted in `bd-wee9a`: the fence is taken
+   only in Connection::open (`pager.rs:5207`), never in the
+   commit-time WAL-append path. The cliff driver is
+   `SimpleTransaction.inner.lock()` in Phase A serializing all
+   writers of the same pager upstream of the GroupCommitQueue.
+3. **4t gained +59%, 8t gained +128%** vs campaign-summary. These
+   gains are broadly distributed across the Apr-24 perf wave
+   (DecrJumpZero/SCopy/AddImm VDBE hot-path promotions, parser
+   interner retention, btree CachedCellSlots widening, mvcc
+   SireadTable short-circuit, planner index-selection metric mutex
+   removal, PublishedPagerState 4096→512 floor, `RecoveryFence`
+   spin-fast-path, etc.) — not attributable to any single commit.
+4. **Peak 2t→4t→8t ratios are still inverted vs sqlite.** sqlite
+   drops from 539k @ 2t to 211k @ 4t to 50k @ 8t (classic fsync
+   contention at high concurrency). fsqlite goes 9.5k → 9.5k → 12k
+   — we're serialized so hard that more threads is *almost free
+   because no work is happening*.
+

+## Disposition
+
+- `bd-cnk5d` (fence spin-fast-path): closed by `0f04cb25`; verified
+  here. No rework needed.
+- `bd-wee9a` (Phase-A inner.lock cliff): stays open. 2t stably
+  pinned at 9,554 wps across three back-to-back runs is the
+  clearest evidence yet that the cliff is deterministic, not
+  scheduling variance. Any follow-up work on the cliff must shrink
+  or bypass Phase A's `self.inner.lock()` hold; the
+  GroupCommitQueue tuning pane is a dead end (already tested
+  empirically, see `0df7d65e` commit body).
+- Candidate next-pager levers from the rank-1-2 profile:
+  - `Arc<ShardedPageCache>::drop_slow` 7.02% — single fattest
+    on-CPU symbol at 2t. Worth a dedicated investigation bead.
+  - `xxh3_64_long_default` 5.31% (page_mutation_counter) —
+    `T5a` experiment in 2026-04-23 reverted the crc32c swap
+    because crc32c was 28 % slower for 4 KiB inputs on this host.
+    Fix requires *avoiding* the hash call, not replacing the
+    primitive.
